title: claude tile data
description: Data object bound to the bundled `claude` tile's template. Feed it as the module's `exec` stdout (JSON) or static `text`. Each property notes whether the value is REAL (available from the claude-status-db daemon) or MOCK (synthesize it; not a session field yet).

shortcut.description: MOCK. The niri desktop's shortcut number, shown first. (Tracked by the daemon's workspace allocator, not a session field.)

state.description: REAL (sessions.state). Drives the animated <status> indicator: working=blinking orange dot, prompt=blinking yellow '?', idle=static fade bar (see idle_level), shell=pulsing cyan dot.

idle_level.description: REAL-derived (from last_talk_ts decay). Only meaningful when state=idle: 0=just idled (bright white) .. 6=>60min (dim grey). Ignored otherwise.

idle_ago.description: REAL-derived (from last_talk_ts). Pre-formatted 'time since active' shown only when state=idle, e.g. '12m' or '2h' (rendered as '12m ago'). You format the string.

folder.description: REAL (basename of sessions.cwd). The session's working-directory name.

title.description: MOCK. The session window title, shown as a looping marquee on line 2. (Resolve from window_id via the niri window model; not stored on the session row.)
examples: Harder Better Faster Stronger · refactor the inline flow layout

unpushed.description: MOCK. Count of commits not yet pushed on the session's repo; shown as ↑N (hidden when 0). Not a daemon field — compute it yourself.

active.description: Whether this is the focused/active niri desktop. When true, the tile gets an accent 'card' (fill + border) to stand out from the others.

is_claude.description: Whether this desktop runs a Claude session (true → the status/folder layout) or an ordinary window (false → the app-icon layout). For a niri desktop, derive from whether the daemon tracks a session on it.

app.description: Used only when is_claude=false: the app/window label shown beside its icon.

app_icon.description: Used only when is_claude=false: the leftmost window's icon. Either a bundled icon name (e.g. 'code', 'terminal') or an absolute path to an .svg (contains '/'), e.g. a freedesktop app icon. The real 'infinite glyphs' case a font can't cover.
